title.purpose: Page section labels, dialog headers. Uppercase + tracking creates catalog hierarchy.

body.purpose: Card descriptions, metadata, explanatory copy. Cap at ~65ch.

label.purpose: Rarity abbreviations, condition tags, language chips. Weight compensates for small size.

mono.purpose: Set codes, card extensions, print identifiers. Distinguishes metadata from names visually.

shadows.name: inset-selected-give
shadows.purpose: Selected trade row in 'You give' column.

shadows.name: inset-selected-recv
shadows.purpose: Selected trade row in 'You receive' column.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions (background, border, shadow).

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Hover lift animations, card enter transitions.

motion.name: duration-base
motion.purpose: Trade row selection, nav item hover.

components.name: Trade Button
components.description: Primary action for trade pile and propose-trade flows. Amethyst fill, white text.
components.css: .ds-btn-trade { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: #C084FC; color: #ffffff; border: none; border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 20px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-trade:hover { background: #B56EFF; } .ds-btn-trade:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #C084FC; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Accent Button
components.description: Wishlist and desire actions. Hot pink fill, white text.
components.css: .ds-btn-accent { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: #F0487A; color: #ffffff; border: none; border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 20px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-accent:hover { background: #FF6A9A; } .ds-btn-accent:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #F0487A; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Mutual Chip
components.description: Mutual match badge. Lime green on dark tinted background. Appears only on confirmed mutual trades.
components.css: .ds-chip-mutual { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: rgba(132,204,22,0.15); color: #84cc16; border: 1px solid rgba(132,204,22,0.3); border-radius: 9999px; padding: 4px 10px; font-size: 0.6875rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.04em; }

components.name: Trade Row
components.description: Interactive card row in the Propose Trade dialog. Toggles between unselected and selected states.
components.html:
✓

Dark Magician

LOB-EN005 · UR · NM
components.css: .ds-trade-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; border: 1px solid #3D1F80; border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 12px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), border-color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), box-shadow 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); user-select: none; } .ds-trade-row:hover { background: #221550; } .ds-trade-row--selected { border-color: rgba(240,72,122,0.5); background: rgba(133,20,75,0.15); box-shadow: inset 0 0 20px rgba(133,20,75,0.08); } .ds-trade-row__check { color: #F0487A; font-size: 14px; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-trade-row__img { height: 72px; width: 50px; object-fit: contain; border-radius: 6px; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-trade-row__info { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; min-width: 0; } .ds-trade-row__name { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; color: #E8E0FF; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; } .ds-trade-row__meta { font-size: 0.75rem; color: #A78BFA; } .ds-trade-row__code { font-family: ui-monospace, monospace; font-weight: 600; }

components.name: Card Tile
components.description: Vertical card tile in Library and Search. Image fills top, metadata + quantity below.
components.html:
Dark Magician

UR
components.css: .ds-card-tile { display: flex; flex-direction: column; border: 1px solid #3D1F80; border-radius: 0 0 8px 8px; overflow: hidden; width: 160px; background: transparent; } .ds-card-tile__img { width: 100%; object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 59 / 86; display: block; } .ds-card-tile__data { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; padding: 8px 12px 4px; } .ds-card-tile__name { font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; color: #E8E0FF; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; line-height: 1.3; } .ds-card-tile__badges { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 4px; } .ds-card-tile__rarity { background: rgba(120,53,15,0.5); border: 1px solid rgba(180,120,50,0.4); color: #fcd34d; font-size: 0.625rem; font-weight: 700; padding: 2px 4px; border-radius: 4px; letter-spacing: 0.04em; }

components.name: Nav Item
components.description: Icon-only navigation item with tooltip, hover background, and active underline indicator.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 2px; cursor: pointer; padding: 4px 8px; border-radius: 6px; transition: background-color 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-nav-item:hover, .ds-nav-item--active { background: #221550; } .ds-nav-item__indicator { height: 2px; width: 16px; border-radius: 9999px; background: transparent; transition: background-color 0.15s; } .ds-nav-item__indicator--active { background: #F0487A; }

narrative.overview:
TradeMarket is built around a specific moment: the back table at a local game store, where two collectors spread their binders and negotiate a trade face to face. That exchange is direct, personal, and driven entirely by mutual need. The UI should feel like that table: honest, focused, slightly dim, with card sleeves glinting under fluorescent light.

The visual register is dark by default — deep indigo-black backgrounds protect against glare fatigue during long collection-building sessions. Color is used with purpose: amethyst for trading actions, hot pink for desire and wishlists, lime green reserved exclusively for the mutual match.

What this system explicitly rejects: the neon maximalism of NFT/crypto marketplaces; the sterile white grid of eBay and TCGPlayer; the sprawling spreadsheet UIs of legacy collection trackers. Cardcluster's functional clarity is the closest public reference.
keyCharacteristics: Dark-first, with a secondary light mode using lavender-tinted whites
Full palette: three semantic color roles (trade/accent/mutual) used with discipline
Card images do the visual work; the UI frames and recedes
Monospace font for set codes and card identifiers
No decorative chrome — every border, shadow, and badge earns its place
Interaction hierarchy is tight: primary → secondary → tertiary, never ambiguous

rules.name: The Three-Role Rule
rules.body: Trade (amethyst), Accent (hot pink), and Mutual (lime) are the only saturated colors in the system. Each maps to exactly one meaning and is never borrowed for decoration.

rules.name: The Mutual Scarcity Rule
rules.body: Lime green appears only for confirmed mutual matches and 'they want this' signals. Never use it for generic success states, positive alerts, or decoration. Its rarity is the point.

rules.name: The No-Gray Rule
rules.body: Pure grays are prohibited. Every neutral is tinted toward the brand hue. Use var(--c-muted) (Soft Violet) for muted text, not a Tailwind gray class.

rules.name: The Mono Identifier Rule
rules.body: Set codes, card extensions, and print codes are always rendered in monospace. This lets the eye distinguish metadata from card names instantly.

rules.name: The Uppercase Section Rule
rules.body: Section headers use uppercase + tracking, never sentence case. This creates a catalog-style hierarchy native to the TCG product category.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. No card has a shadow unless it is in an interactive hover state. Hover glow uses the element's semantic color at low opacity, never a gray shadow.

dos: Do use var(--c-trade) for all giving actions, var(--c-accent) for all wanting actions, and var(--c-mutual) only for confirmed mutual matches.
Do render set codes and card extensions in monospace. The visual difference from card names matters to collectors.
Do use loading='lazy' and aspect-ratio: 59/86 on every card image to prevent layout shift.
Do use transition: background-color, border-color, box-shadow — explicit properties only. Never transition: all.
Do abbreviate rarities to initials in compact contexts and show the full name on hover via title attribute.
Do keep all interactive elements keyboard-reachable with visible focus rings.
Do use the three-surface tonal stack (--c-bg → --c-surface → --c-surface-2) for depth instead of shadows at rest.
donts: Don't use pure gray (#555, #666, text-gray-400, color='#ccc'). Use var(--c-muted) instead.
Don't use transition: all. Always name the CSS properties explicitly.
Don't build a loud neon aesthetic — no rainbow gradients, no glowing borders on every element.
Don't create sterile white product grids. The light mode uses lavender-tinted whites, never clinical white.
Don't use side-stripe borders (border-left > 1px as a colored accent). Use full borders, background tints, or leading icons instead.
Don't use background-clip: text with a gradient background. Emphasis through weight and size contrast only.
Don't render the mutual color for anything other than confirmed mutual matches.
Don't place headless AddCard components inside Vuetify v-overlay default slots — they teleport out of DOM scope and break $refs.
Don't mix script setup with Options API methods that need $refs access without defineExpose().
